assumptions: The intragroup dividend has been declared and recognised as a monetary receivable/payable between group entities with different functional currencies.

The related foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation, so the intragroup monetary item can qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements under the foreign-currency exception for intragroup items.
If a net investment hedge is considered, the group includes a foreign operation whose net assets are included in the consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This is relevant because a recognised intragroup dividend receivable/payable is a monetary asset or liability, and its foreign currency risk can be documented in consolidation if the FX effects are not fully eliminated.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This is relevant because IFRS 9 includes cash flow hedges for foreign currency risk associated with recognised assets or liabilities when the FX-driven cash flow variability could affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This is relevant if the group documents the foreign currency exposure at the level of a foreign operation's net investment rather than only at the level of the intragroup receivable.
